source_title: DOW-UAP-D38, Range Fouler Debrief, Middle East, May 2020
description_blurb:
This document is a Range Fouler Debrief, a standardized reporting form the U.S. Navy uses to record the circumstances surrounding an unauthorized intrusion into controlled airspace during active military operations or training. These reports contain a narrative description of the observer’s experiences. 
A U.S. military operator reported observing a “solid white object [fly] through the [field-of-view]. The reporter described the UAP as making erratic [movements] above the water.
All descriptive and estimative language contained in this report reflects the reporter’s subjective interpretation at the time of the event. Such characterizations should not be interpreted as a conclusive indication of the presence or absence of any intrinsic object features or performance characteristics.
title: DOW-UAP-D38, Range Fouler Debrief, Middle East, May 2020
summary: A U.S. military operator performing an ISR tasking observed a solid white, round, opaque object fly through the field-of-view. The object was temporarily lost but re-acquired, and the crew was able to follow it as it made erratic movements above the water, achieving 4x zoom before losing the object due to poor track placement. The sensor operator continuously manipulated the sensor to maintain tracking.
reported_object_description: A solid white, round, opaque object.
reported_behavior: The object flew through the field-of-view, made erratic movements above the water, was temporarily lost and re-acquired, and was lost due to poor track placement. It was moving.
bust_reasoning: The document describes a multi-sensor (infrared, intermittent radar) and multi-witness observation of a 'solid white, round, opaque object' exhibiting 'erratic movements above the water.' The object was tracked, zoomed on, and required continuous sensor manipulation. The 'erratic movements' are a key feature that resists easy explanation for a simple balloon or debris, pushing the score below 50. However, the description does not indicate behavior incompatible with known craft, placing it in the 15-34 range for genuinely uncertain observations with unusual features.
full_text:
# DOW-UAP-D38, Range Fouler Debrief, Middle East, May 2020 - Page 1

Declassified by MG Richard A. Harrison
USCENTCOM Chief of Staff
Please do not use the purple "submit" button above. Save and email the complete file manually (see below).

# Range Fouler Debrief Form

Please complete this form to the best of your ability. If you do not have the requested information, please leave the field blank. If there was more than one "group," please report each on a separate form for data collection purposes. You should receive a response within 5 business days.

| Last Name, First Name | Rank | Squadron | SIPR Email Address | Crew Position |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| | | [ ] | | |

This information is for contact only. SPEAR sanitizes all reports of identifying information. Absolutely no identifying information for aircrew or squadron will be recorded for analysis.

| 05/14/20 | 20:40:00 | Night | | | ISR | |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| Date (mm/dd/yy) | Time of detection (hh:mm:ss Z) | Day / Night | Side No. | Buno | Mission Description (CAS, BFM, etc) | LFE? |

| 28314 | 28314 | N | 49524 | E | 20000 | Yes | | |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| Contact Working Area (Ex: W-72 1A) | Contact Latitute (DD:MM:SS) | N/S | Contact Longitude (DD:MM:SS) | E/W | Contact Altitude (Ex: 22000) | Altitude Constant? | Wind Dir at Contact Alt (From) | Wind Speed |

Please attempt to determine a Lat / Long for the contact at initial detection. We recommend using JMPS to reference the sensor-derived bearing and range relative to the bullseye you had in use. If that method is not available, any reasonable derivation of location will work, but please be as specific as possible and make a note in the comments section. These locations may be used to cue other means of tracking.

Was the contact moving? **Yes**
Direction/Speed (Ex. 090/15)

Radar Equipped: [ ]
Stable Trackfile?: Intermittent
# of Contacts in "Group": 1

AIM-9x Self-Track: [ ]
ATFLIR Autotrack: [ ]
Tally Achieved: [ ]

EA Indications (Check all that apply):
[ ] ECM Arc | [ ] Letter Identifier | [ ] False Trackfiles | [ ] Other/Ambiguous

Please check all that apply:
[x] Round | [ ] Moving Parts:
[ ] Square | [ ] Metallic:
[ ] Balloon-shaped: | [ ] Markings:
[ ] Wings/Airframe: | [ ] Translucent:
[ ] Other Shape: | [ ] Opaque:
[ ] Apparent Propulsion: | [ ] Reflective:

Please use the field below to describe the contact and any interaction in your own words with as much detail as possible. Please be sure to include any detail not included in questions above.

While preforming an ISR tasking (ULTN/Black Hot/Lin), a solid white object flew through the FOV. There was a temporarily lose of the object but re-acquired shortly there after. The crew was able to follow the object as it appeared to make erratic moments above the water. During the follow, crew was able to obtain 4x zoom on the object but lost the object due to poor track placement. While following, the sensor operator was continuously manipulating the sensor to maintain eyes on the object. This is apparent by the waves of the water in the background being visible and not being visible.

Don't use the purple "submit" button! Save this form with filename "Date_Squadron_RF.pdf" and email it to [redacted] (Also in the global). For troubleshooting, call [redacted].

Thank you for your time. Please ensure all display tapes are ripped for the entire time of interaction and saved as a .wmv (Example: 4 May VFA-106 HUD.wmv). Squadron intel personnel shall upload those files to the repository located at this link.

[redacted]
USCENTCOM MDR 26-0019 | Approved for Release to AARO | 01/26/26 001
cover_up_reasoning: The document exhibits routine redaction of personally identifiable information (PII) for the aircrew and squadron, as well as administrative contact details (email addresses, phone numbers, repository links). There are no references to classified annexes, no substantial black-bar redactions of the narrative, and no indications of witness intimidation or contradictions with public statements. The document explicitly states 'Approved for Release to AARO,' indicating a standard declassification and release process.
title_zh: DOW-UAP-D38，靶场干扰物情况汇报，中东，2020年5月
summary_zh: 一名美国军事操作员在执行ISR任务时，观察到一个实心白色、圆形、不透明物体飞过视野。该物体曾短暂丢失但被重新捕获，机组人员能够对其进行跟踪，观察到其在水面上方进行不规则移动，并达到4倍变焦，之后因跟踪定位不佳而丢失该物体。传感器操作员持续操控传感器以保持跟踪。
reported_object_description_zh: 一个实心白色、圆形、不透明物体。
reported_behavior_zh: 该物体飞过视野，在水面上方进行不规则移动，曾短暂丢失但被重新捕获，之后因跟踪定位不佳而丢失。其处于移动状态。
bust_reasoning_zh: 该文件描述了一项多传感器（红外、间歇性雷达）和多目击者的观察，针对一个表现出“在水面上方进行不规则移动”的“实心白色、圆形、不透明物体”。该物体被跟踪、变焦，并需要持续操控传感器。“不规则移动”是一个关键特征，难以用简单的气球或碎片来解释，因此评分低于50。然而，该描述并未表明其行为与已知飞行器不符，因此将其置于15-34分区间，代表具有异常特征的真正不确定观察。
cover_up_reasoning_zh: 该文件对机组人员和中队的个人身份信息（PII）以及行政联系方式（电子邮件地址、电话号码、存储库链接）进行了例行编辑。文件中没有提及机密附件，没有对叙述进行实质性的黑条编辑，也没有任何证人恐吓或与公开声明相矛盾的迹象。该文件明确指出“批准向AARO发布”，表明这是一个标准的解密和发布流程。
claims: A solid white, round, opaque object was observed.
The object made erratic movements above the water.
The object was temporarily lost and re-acquired.
The crew achieved 4x zoom on the object.
The object was lost due to poor track placement.
claims_zh: 观察到一个实心白色、圆形、不透明物体。
该物体在水面上方进行不规则移动。
该物体曾短暂丢失但被重新捕获。
机组人员对该物体实现了4倍变焦。
该物体因跟踪定位不佳而丢失。
bust_explanations: Small drone (e.g., consumer or military reconnaissance drone). The 'erratic movements above the water' and 'moving' behavior, combined with multi-sensor detection (IR, intermittent radar), are consistent with a small, maneuverable drone. The 'solid white, round, opaque' description could fit certain drone designs or payloads.

Weather balloon or similar atmospheric balloon. The 'solid white, round, opaque' description fits. However, 'erratic movements above the water' and the need for continuous sensor manipulation to track it make a simple, wind-driven balloon less straightforward, unless it was caught in complex air currents.

Misidentified conventional object (e.g., marine debris, buoy, or a very small, unusual aircraft). While 'erratic movements' and 'moving' are noted, the 'round, solid white, opaque' signature and multi-sensor tracking make it less likely to be common debris. A very small, unconventional aircraft could fit, but the document provides no further details.
bust_explanations_zh: 小型无人机（例如，消费级或军用侦察无人机）。“在水面上方进行不规则移动”和“移动”的行为，结合多传感器探测（红外、间歇性雷达），与小型、机动性强的无人机一致。“实心白色、圆形、不透明”的描述可能符合某些无人机设计或有效载荷。
气象气球或类似大气气球。“实心白色、圆形、不透明”的描述符合。然而，“在水面上方进行不规则移动”以及需要持续操控传感器进行跟踪，使得简单的、随风飘动的气球解释不那么直接，除非它被卷入复杂的空气气流中。
被误识别的常规物体（例如，海洋碎片、浮标或非常小的、不寻常的飞行器）。尽管记录了“不规则移动”和“移动”，但“圆形、实心白色、不透明”的特征和多传感器跟踪使其不太可能是普通碎片。一个非常小的、非常规飞行器可能符合，但该文件未提供进一步细节。
cover_up_indicators: Redaction of PII for aircrew and squadron
Redaction of administrative contact information (email, phone, repository link)
cover_up_indicators_zh: 对机组人员和中队PII的编辑
对行政联系信息（电子邮件、电话、存储库链接）的编辑
